opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I started new antibiotics yesterday and tonight my arms are covered in little blisters. I'm terrified my skin is going to peel off.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The blisters came up fast and I'm scared it's a severe drug reaction.

symptom_narrative.character: small tense clear blisters, some in ring-like clusters, mildly itchy
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no mouth/eye/genital sores, no lip swelling, no trouble breathing, no fever, no skin peeling in sheets

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a new oral antibiotic (started ~48 h ago for a skin infection)

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: wants to know if her skin will peel off

objective_data_offered.value: small tense clear blisters on both forearms, some arranged in ring-like clusters ('string of pearls'); mouth/eyes/genitals look normal; no skin peeling when rubbed
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: phone-camera view; cannot perform Nikolsky testing or biopsy remotely
